title: Weak Agent Lifecycle Mutation Safety
goal: Resolve #405 by strengthening lifecycle mutation safety coverage and review-required signals before weak-agent-facing lifecycle expansion.
non_goals: Do not generate lifecycle mutation adapters.
Do not hide destructive risk from agents.
Do not replace existing module-local lifecycle tests.

intent.outcome: High-risk lifecycle mutation scenarios are covered by black-box fixtures or a concrete fixture plan, and weak agents see review-required signals before unsafe mutation.
intent.constraints: Keep mutation/apply primitives hand-owned; prefer black-box workspace CLI checks over implementation-specific unit tests.
intent.latitude: Add compact risk/review output only when existing surfaces do not clearly separate dry-run, apply, strict preflight, ownership refusal, or local-only preservation.
intent.escalation: Escalate if the fix requires generated lifecycle mutation adapters or broad lifecycle primitive rewrites.

execution.next_step: Archive this plan, update external issue evidence, close #405, then promote the next child slice.
execution.proof: Focused lifecycle tests, package proof selector, full workspace CLI tests, ruff, planning doctor, summary, and reconcile passed.

invariants: Dry-run and apply lifecycle paths remain visibly separate.
Local-only memory and integration surfaces are preserved unless local-only removal is explicitly requested.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Improve weak-agent recovery and strong-agent leverage for package lifecycle and closeout workflows.

required_continuation.activation trigger: After #405 is validated, promote the next open child slice in the weak-agent recovery lane.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Weak-agent lifecycle mutation safety is visible in lifecycle_plan.mutation_safety and covered by black-box fixtures.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Validation-vs-intent broad closeout remains the later child slice in this lane.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: The local-only lifecycle plan apply command used the wrong flag name; fixed to --local-only.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: Focused lifecycle tests, full workspace CLI tests, ruff, package proof selector, planning doctor, summary, and reconcile passed.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: Promote the next weak-agent recovery child slice from state.toml.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Ingest and implement all issues lane by lane. Commit and push after each milestone. Collect dogfooding evidence.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Continue the weak-agent lane by closing #405 with checked-in planning, proof, and continuation evidence.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Implement #405 as a bounded lifecycle safety coverage and compact signal slice.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm uninstall/refusal, local-only preservation, dry-run/apply separation, and strict preflight/review signals are explicitly covered.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: workspace CLI tests/contracts/output and planning evidence surfaces for #405
execution_bounds.required validation commands: focused lifecycle tests, full workspace CLI tests, ruff, planning doctor, summary, reconcile
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Any lifecycle primitive rewrite, generated mutation adapter work, or package-local installer refactor.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Module-local installer internals unless black-box coverage proves a real behavior gap that cannot be covered at workspace level.

context_budget.live working set: issue #405, lifecycle readiness contract, lifecycle tests, workspace CLI lifecycle output
context_budget.recoverable later: GitHub issue body and archived plan closeout
context_budget.externalize before shift: Keep this plan updated with fixture coverage and closeout state.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Active slice #405: strengthen weak-agent lifecycle mutation safety coverage.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Mutation semantics change, fixture scope widens, or lifecycle adapter generation becomes tempting.

references.label: Expand lifecycle mutation safety before weak-agent use

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Make lifecycle mutation safety stronger before weak-agent-facing expansion.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Keep mutation/apply primitives hand-owned and do not generate lifecycle mutation adapters.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact fixture shape and compact output wording, as long as priority risks are covered or explicitly planned.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The implementation needs lifecycle primitive rewrites, generated mutation adapters, or broad package-local refactors.

active_milestone.scope: Lifecycle mutation risk fixture coverage and compact review-required signals.

immediate_next_action: Inspect current lifecycle readiness and tests, then add the smallest missing fixture coverage or compact signal.
invariants: Dry-run and apply lifecycle paths remain visibly separate.
Local-only memory and integration surfaces are preserved unless local-only removal is explicitly requested.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "lifecycle or uninstall or upgrade or strict_preflight or local_only"
uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed tests/test_workspace_cli.py src/agentic_workspace/cli.py src/agentic_workspace/contracts/lifecycle_generation_readiness.json --format json
uv run ruff check src tests
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: High-risk lifecycle scenarios have fixture coverage or a concrete fixture plan.
Uninstall/refusal and local-only preservation are explicitly covered.
Dry-run and apply remain clearly separated.
Weak agents get review-required signals before unsafe mutation.
